Description: Synchronous inline <script> in <body> blocks HTML parsing and rendering. The script executes immediately, delaying First Contentful Paint (FCP) and Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).
Recommendation: Move the script to an external file and load with defer or async attribute, or place at the very end of <body> with defer. For non-critical scripts, use async or dynamic import().

Description: Using innerHTML triggers HTML parsing and can cause reflows. It also introduces XSS vulnerability when combined with user-controlled input (location.hash).
Recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML to avoid XSS risk and reduce parsing overhead. Sanitize any user input before DOM insertion.

Description: The page has no external scripts loaded with defer or async. The inline script blocks rendering without any loading optimization.
Recommendation: Externalize scripts and use <script defer src='...'> to allow HTML parsing to continue while scripts download in parallel.

Description: The @font-face declaration for 'CustomFont' does not include font-display: swap (or optional/fallback). This causes Flash of Invisible Text (FOIT) where text is hidden until the custom font loads, hurting FCP and LCP.
Recommendation: Add font-display: swap to the @font-face rule so a fallback system font is shown immediately while the custom font loads.

Description: The custom font file (/fonts/custom.woff2) is not preloaded. The browser discovers it only after parsing CSS, adding latency to font loading and delaying text rendering.
Recommendation: Add <link rel='preload' href='/fonts/custom.woff2' as='font' type='font/woff2' crossorigin> in the <head> to start downloading the font earlier.

Description: Only woff2 format is provided. While woff2 has wide support, older browsers may not render the custom font at all without a woff fallback.
Recommendation: Consider adding a woff fallback source for broader compatibility: src: url('custom.woff2') format('woff2'), url('custom.woff') format('woff').

Description: The synchronous <link rel='stylesheet'> in <head> blocks rendering until the entire CSS file downloads from cdn.example.com. This directly delays LCP.
Recommendation: Inline critical CSS for above-the-fold content and load the full stylesheet asynchronously using <link rel='preload' as='style' onload='this.rel="stylesheet"'>. Consider self-hosting critical CSS.

Issue: No resource preloading for LCP image
Description: The hero image (/public/hero.png) is likely the LCP element but is not preloaded. The browser discovers it only when parsing the HTML tag.
Recommendation: Add <link rel='preload' as='image' href='/public/hero.png'> in <head> to begin downloading the LCP image earlier.

Description: The hero image uses PNG format (800x400). Modern formats like WebP or AVIF offer 25-50% smaller file sizes, improving LCP.
Recommendation: Convert hero.png to WebP or AVIF and use with fallback: .

Issue: Image missing width and height attributes
Description: The promo.png tag lacks explicit width and height attributes. The browser cannot reserve space before the image loads, causing layout shifts (CLS).
Recommendation: Add explicit width and height attributes to the tag, or use CSS aspect-ratio to reserve space.

Description: Even with font-display: swap (once added), swapping from a fallback to the custom font can cause layout shift if metrics differ.
Recommendation: Use CSS size-adjust, ascent-override, and descent-override in the @font-face rule to match fallback font metrics, or use the font-metric-overrides approach.

Description: Inline synchronous JS on the main thread can block user interaction responsiveness. If this script grows or triggers reflows, it impacts Interaction to Next Paint (INP).
Recommendation: Defer non-critical JavaScript execution. Use requestIdleCallback or setTimeout for non-urgent DOM updates.
